input.title: Ride Sharing Application
input.description: Ride Sharing Application is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React Native and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Rider Registration
requirements: Register with phone number and OTP
Add profile with name and profile photo

FR-02.title: Driver Registration
requirements: Register with personal and vehicle details
Upload driving license and vehicle registration

FR-04.title: Driver Matching
requirements: Match nearest available driver to rider
Driver accepts or declines within 30 seconds
Show driver details and ETA on acceptance

FR-05.title: Real-time Trip Tracking
requirements: Share trip status with emergency contacts

FR-06.title: Fare Calculation
requirements: Calculate fare based on distance and time

FR-07.title: Payment Processing
requirements: Support card, wallet, and cash payments

FR-08.title: Rating and Feedback
requirements: Rider rates driver after trip (1-5 stars)

FR-09.title: Driver Earnings Dashboard
requirements: View daily, weekly, and monthly earnings

FR-11.title: Promo and Referral System
requirements: Apply promo codes for ride discounts
Referral program with credits for both parties

FR-01.title: Plan Rider Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for rider registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Register with phone number and OTP; Add profile with name and profile photo; Save payment methods.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for rider registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for rider registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for rider registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Rider Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for rider registration on React Native, Go, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Register with phone number and OTP; Add profile with name and profile photo; Save payment methods.
acceptance_criteria: Core rider registration business rules are implemented for Ride Sharing Application
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for rider registration
API/service behavior for rider registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Rider Registration Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for rider registration with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The rider registration user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for rider registration behave correctly
The rider registration UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Rider Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by rider registration, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by rider registration are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for rider registration integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for rider registration are handled securely

FR-01.title: Harden Rider Registration Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to rider registration. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for rider registration are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in rider registration are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for rider registration passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Rider Registration Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for rider registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for rider registration are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for rider registration are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical rider registration scenarios
